era_context: Early Iron Age II. Ashur-dan II of Assyria continued his reign. In the Levant, the traditional period of Solomon's rule continued (though disputed). Egypt remained fragmented under the late 21st Dynasty. Greece was in the Geometric period. The Zhou Dynasty in China was in its middle period, with regional states becoming increasingly autonomous.

events.title: Ashur-dan II's continued reign and consolidation of Assyrian power
events.description: Ashur-dan II (r. 934–912 BCE) continued his efforts to rebuild Assyrian military and political power during this year. His reign marked the beginning of Assyrian resurgence after a period of decline, setting the stage for the later Neo-Assyrian Empire. The specific events of 961 BCE are not individually documented in surviving sources.
events.certainty_note: Ashur-dan II's reign is well-established, but specific events within individual years are not reliably documented.

disconfirming_evidence: No specific disconfirming evidence for 961 BCE. The broader issue is that Assyrian chronology in this period, while relatively secure, still contains uncertainties of ±5 years in some scholarly systems.
historiographic_note: The record for 961 BCE is almost entirely dependent on Assyrian royal inscriptions. No other contemporary sources document events in this year. The absence of evidence from other regions reflects the limitations of surviving documentation and the Mesopotamian focus of available sources.

graph_edges.note: Part of the same reign period as documented in -964-001.
